# Section 04B — Ecosystem Activity Methodology

> **All source URLs (site-wide):** [`../../DATA_REFERENCES.md`](../../DATA_REFERENCES.md) — [DS-07](../../DATA_REFERENCES.md#ds-07-section-04b-business--social-ecosystem-constellation) · [04B appendix](../../DATA_REFERENCES.md#section-04b--business--social-ecosystem-constellation).

**Access date:** Generated by `build_section_04b_activity_from_sources.py`  
**Visualization layer:** Editorial constellation (not live analytics)

## Purpose

Support the cinematic ecosystem network with **reproducible, source-backed indicators** while keeping the UI abstract (Low → High signal labels, not raw event feeds).

## Data sources (v1)

| ID | Source | Method | Used for |
|----|--------|--------|----------|
| SRC-OSM-OVERPASS | OpenStreetMap via Overpass | Point counts in Boston neighborhoods + corridor bboxes | Cafes, restaurants, bookstores, coworking, social POI totals |
| SRC-ENRICHMENT | `ecosystem_poi_supplements_v1.json` | Documented public directory **floors** (max with OSM) | Coworking/cultural gaps where OSM under-tags active corridors |
| SRC-INNOVATION-DIRECTORY | Innovation district / coworking listings | Curated hub/geo floors | Kendall, Seaport, Downtown innovation offices |
| SRC-CULTURAL-VENUE-REF | Bookstore & library venue references | Curated hub/geo floors | Harvard Square, Back Bay, Porter Square literary anchors |
| SRC-MEETUP-GROUP | Meetup group registry (`corridor_registry.json`) | Linked community groups per corridor | Meetup/community signal boost (documented slugs, not live API) |
| SRC-MIT-ENGAGE-ICAL | [MIT Events iCal](https://engage.mit.edu/ical/mit/ical_mit.ics) | Download & cache ICS; parse `VEVENT`; match to corridors by title/location keywords | University event density, innovation/cultural keyword counts, hover highlights |
| SRC-MEETUP-GROUP | Meetup.com (documented groups) | Registry of public group slugs; **no API key** in v1 | Documented in corridor registry for future ICS/HTML pulls |

### Not used in v1 (blocked or API-key required)

- Harvard `events.harvard.edu` iCal (403 without MITnet)
- Eventbrite API (token required)
- Meetup API (OAuth / deprecated public endpoints)

## Corridor matching (MIT events)

Each corridor defines `keywords` in `data/section_04/raw/activity/corridor_registry.json`.  
An MIT event is **matched** if any keyword appears in `SUMMARY`, `LOCATION`, `CATEGORIES`, or `DESCRIPTION` (case-insensitive).

## Signal derivation

Per corridor, compute:

1. **OSM social POI total** — cafes + restaurants (neighborhood sum or corridor bbox CSV)
2. **MIT matched events** — count of keyword-matched iCal events
3. **Keyword subsets** — innovation / student / cultural / networking regex flags on matched events

Composite scores (0–1) combine OSM + event counts. Signals (`low` → `high`) are **quartile ranks across all 22 corridors** so the visualization stays relative and balanced.

`activity_index` = mean of the four signal level scores (used for subtle hub glow only).

## Outputs

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `raw/activity/feeds/*.ics` | Cached downloads |
| `processed/activity_evidence_v1.json` | Audit log per corridor |
| `processed/layer_b_ecosystem_activity_v1.csv` | Site-facing signals |
| `website/data/section_04b_ecosystem_activity.csv` | Published copy |

## Rebuild

```bash
# OSM layers (if needed)
python3 data/scripts/build_section_04_business_osm.py
python3 data/scripts/build_section_04_corridor_osm.py

# Activity signals
python3 data/scripts/build_section_04b_activity_from_sources.py
```

## Academic honesty note

MIT iCal is **region-wide** (all public MIT events), not geocoded to corridors. Keyword matching is a **transparent proxy** for “ecosystem activity near this corridor,” not a precise spatial join. OSM POIs are the strongest spatially grounded layer.
